title: Flame, Fuel, Farm, and Forge
description: A Minecraft mod that adds realism, challenge, and historical flavor.
body:
**Flame, Fuel, Farm & Forge** (FFFF) is a lightweight realism-focused mod inspired by a coding class project. It began as an experiment to make torches burn out — and quickly grew into a full early-game progression overhaul, adding tin, bronze, lantern fueling, animal fat, accurate ore names, and more.

This mod is great for players who enjoy:

- Survival challenge
- Base-building
- Historical immersion
- Automation experiments
- Early-game resource progression

## Torches Burn Out

Regular and copper torches now behave more realistically:

Torch Lifespans

- Regular Torch: ~1 Minecraft day
- Copper Torch: ~3 Minecraft days

When a torch burns out, it becomes a Burnt Torch:

- Mining a burnt torch drops ashes (nothing)
- Mining a torch before it burns out drops Kindling
- Kindling works as a low-grade furnace fuel

## Torches Cause Fire Damage

Torches are now mildly dangerous:

- Walking through a torch’s hitbox can cause small fire damage
- Mobs take the same damage
- Hitting a creature with a torch ignites it — works for all torch types
- Regular torches can ignite flammable blocks they’re placed on
- Copper torches do not ignite blocks
- Anthracite (coal ore) is flammable — place carefully!

## Smarter World-Gen Lighting

Village torches and other world-generated torches are replaced with:

- Copper Torches, or
- Lanterns (preferred when placement allows)

Copper torches from world-gen can still burn out naturally.

## Animals Drop Fat

Some animals now drop Animal Fat, used for:

- Furnace fuel
- Lantern fuel
- Oil production

## Lantern Fueling

Lanterns now require fuel:

- All Lanterns
  - Have one internal fuel slot
  - Holds 16 units of fuel (Oil or Animal Fat)
- 1 Animal Fat = 1 day of light
- 1 Bottle of Oil = 3 days of light

Bronze Hoppers are included:

- 3 slots (smaller capacity)
- Slower than iron hoppers

## Copper Kettle

A new container similar to a cauldron, but:

- Max capacity 2
- Cannot hold hot liquids
- Accepts Animal Fat, Oil, or Water

Placed over heat → converts Animal Fat → Oil

Bonus:
- Composters now drip Oil whenever they produce bone meal. A Copper Kettle or Cauldron placed beneath catches the oil.

## Ore items have been renamed for historical realism:

- Raw Copper → Malachite
- Coal → Anthracite

The functionality is exactly the same, they are just renamed.

## Tin, Bronze & Early Tools

Adds Cassiterite Ore → Tin Ingots, craftable into nuggets and blocks.

### Bronze

Combining tin + copper allows crafting:

- Bronze Tools
- Bronze Weapons
- Bronze Armor
- Bronze Blocks / Ingots / Nuggets

Copper equipment is disabled (historically inaccurate).
Stone swords, pickaxes, and shovels are disabled (unrealistic tools).

### Brass

We have added Sphalerite Ore, which can be smelted in a Blast Furnace into Zinc Ingots.

For now you can't do much with it, but we have massive plans for the Brass Age future. Think steampunk!
